one_line_description: Embeddable ecommerce platform (Ecwid by Lightspeed) that adds a store to any website, with a REST API.
auth_summary: Public apps use OAuth2 (code exchanged via POST to my.ecwid.com/api/oauth/token) to obtain access tokens; every app also has non-expiring private (read/write) and public (read-only) access tokens, sent as Bearer tokens. Store owners integrating their own store can skip the OAuth flow and use the token directly.
access_notes: Developers register an app via the Ecwid developer portal and get tokens self-serve; installing on your own store yields an access token immediately. REST API access generally requires the store to be on a paid Ecwid plan.
api_surface_summary: REST API covers products, categories, orders, customers, discount coupons, store profile/settings, and webhooks.
agent_notes: Docs have migrated from api-docs.ecwid.com to docs.ecwid.com. Only community MCP presence found (a Pipedream-powered Ecwid MCP listed in an awesome-mcp-servers registry); no vendor MCP. The paid-plan requirement for API access is a common caveat I could not re-confirm from the current official docs in this pass — flagging as minor uncertainty.
